evals.name: nextjs-chat
evals.prompt: Users are finding ways to jailbreak our chatbot and it's costing us a fortune in API calls. Can you lock it down?
evals.expected_output: Installs @arcjet/next, creates a shared Arcjet client, adds detectPromptInjection(), sensitiveInfo(), tokenBucket(), and detectBot() via withRule(). Calls protect() with sensitiveInfoValue and detectPromptInjectionMessage before streamText(). Returns errors before opening stream.

evals.name: express-openai
evals.prompt: We need to stop people from sending credit card numbers and personal info through our AI chat endpoint.
evals.expected_output: Installs @arcjet/node, adds sensitiveInfo() with deny list for PII types, passes user message as sensitiveInfoValue to protect(). Calls protect() before opening the SSE stream. Handles denied decisions before res.write().

evals.name: fastapi-langchain
evals.prompt: I'm worried about prompt injection attacks on my LangChain chatbot. What can I do?
evals.expected_output: Installs the arcjet pypi package, creates an Arcjet client with detect_prompt_injection() and detect_bot() rules, calls protect() with detect_prompt_injection_message before starting the LangChain stream. Returns error response before opening StreamingResponse.

evals.name: nextjs-existing
evals.prompt: We already have Arcjet set up for rate limiting but now we need AI-specific protections on our chat endpoint — prompt injection and PII filtering.
evals.expected_output: Reuses the existing lib/arcjet.ts client, imports detectPromptInjection and sensitiveInfo, adds them via withRule() in the route file. Passes sensitiveInfoValue and detectPromptInjectionMessage to protect(). Does NOT create a new Arcjet instance.
